Implement the following changes in TingleTap. Do NOT change the UI theme, styling, color palette, typography, animations, Firebase collections, Firestore/RTDB rules, database schema, moderation logic, trust system, or any unrelated functionality.

The implementation must reuse the existing moderation system and existing Firebase data. Avoid unnecessary Firestore/RTDB reads, writes, listeners, or duplicate queries.

---

1. Owner-Only Admin Panel

Restrict the existing AdminPanelPage so that it is accessible only by the Owner.

Requirements:

- Only the Owner role can open AdminPanelPage.
- Admins, Moderators, Premium users, Members, Guests, and every other role must be denied access.
- If a non-owner manually enters the AdminPanel URL, block access and redirect using the existing authorization flow.
- Do not expose Admin Panel data to unauthorized users.

Remove every visible entry point to AdminPanelPage for non-owners, including:

- RoomListPage
- Settings Sidebar → Account
- Navigation menus
- Buttons
- Shortcuts
- Any other existing Admin Panel link

Admins and Moderators should not even see that the Admin Panel exists.

---

2. Create a New Ban/Kick/Mute Panel

Create a new moderation page called:

Ban / Kick / Mute Panel

This is NOT a replacement for AdminPanelPage.

It is a lightweight moderation interface that reuses the existing moderation backend.

Use the exact same TingleTap design system:

- Same layout
- Same theme
- Same spacing
- Same cards
- Same responsive behavior
- Same shadows
- Same typography
- Same button styles
- Same loading states

It must look like it was originally built with the project.

---

3. Access

Allow access only to:

- Owner
- Admin

Do NOT allow:

- Moderator
- Premium
- Members
- Guests
- Any other role

Hide navigation links for unauthorized users.

Protect both frontend routing and existing authorization checks.

---

4. Live Users List

Display one searchable list containing all users.

Include:

- Registered users
- Guests
  - Purush
  - Stree
  - Navrang

Every row should display:

- Profile picture
- Display name
- Real name (if already available)
- Username
- Badge (if available)
- User role
- Guest type
- Current room
- Online / Offline status
- Current moderation status

Reuse existing user/profile data.

Do not create duplicate user documents.

---

5. Moderation Controls

Every row should contain:

- Ban / Unban
- Kick / Unkick (reflect the current kick state if applicable)
- Mute / Unmute

Buttons must always reflect the user's current state.

If a user is moderated from:

- Chat message menu
- User list
- Profile modal
- Existing Admin Panel
- Ban/Kick/Mute Panel

the button state must instantly update everywhere without refreshing.

---

6. Moderation Details

For moderated users display existing information only:

- Current status
- Action performed
- Reason
- Moderator/Admin (if already stored)
- Timestamp
- Expiry time (if temporary)

Reuse existing moderation records.

Do not duplicate moderation data.

---

7. Search & Filters

Support fast filtering by:

- Username
- Display name
- Real name
- Guest
- Registered
- Owner
- Admin
- Moderator
- Online
- Offline
- Muted
- Banned
- Kicked
- Current room

Filtering should not create additional Firebase listeners.

---

8. Firebase Performance (Important)

Keep Firebase usage as low as possible.

Requirements:

- Reuse existing listeners.
- Do NOT create duplicate Firestore listeners.
- Do NOT create duplicate RTDB listeners.
- Do NOT poll Firestore.
- Do NOT repeatedly fetch the users collection.
- Use memoization where appropriate.
- Use efficient queries with limits where possible.
- Reuse already-loaded user/profile/moderation data whenever available.
- Update only the affected document after moderation.
- Do not reload the entire users collection after every action.
- Avoid unnecessary Firestore writes.
- Avoid unnecessary RTDB writes.
- Preserve the current optimized architecture.

The new panel should add minimal additional Firebase cost.

---

9. Icons

Use a professional SVG icon library only.

Use existing project icons if available (preferred).

If new icons are required, use SVG icons from the project's existing icon library (such as Lucide React or React Icons, whichever the project already uses).

Do NOT use:

- Emoji icons
- Unicode icons
- Text symbols

Every action button must use clean SVG icons.

---

10. Preserve Existing Logic

Do NOT modify:

- AI moderation
- Trust system
- Spam detection
- Flood detection
- Ban logic
- Kick logic
- Mute logic
- Reports
- Firebase collections
- Firestore rules
- RTDB rules
- Existing UI behavior

Only build a new moderation interface that reuses the current backend.

---

11. Final Verification

Before considering the task complete:

- AdminPanelPage is accessible only to Owner.
- Admins cannot access AdminPanelPage even via direct URL.
- Moderators cannot access AdminPanelPage.
- Only Owner and Admin can access the new Ban/Kick/Mute Panel.
- Button states remain synchronized across the application.
- No duplicate Firebase listeners have been introduced.
- No unnecessary Firestore or RTDB reads/writes have been added.
- The project builds successfully ("npm run build") with no new warnings or errors.
</user_message>

Messages:
  - Based on the exploration of the codebase, here is a comprehensive report on the routing, access control, and implementation of the Admin Panel and moderation features.

### 1. Routing & Access Control
*   **Routing:** The `AdminPanelPage` is lazily loaded and defined in `src/App.jsx` at the path `/admin-panel`.
    ```javascript
    const AdminPanelPage = lazy(() => import('./pages/AdminPanelPage'));
    // Route in App.jsx:
    <Route path="/admin-panel" element={<ProtectedRoute><Suspense fallback={<AdminLoading />}><AdminPanelPage /></Suspense></ProtectedRoute>} />
    ```
*   **Role-Based Access (Gating):**
    *   **Page Level:** In `AdminPanelPage.jsx`, a listener on the user's Firestore document checks the `role` field. If the role is not `owner` or `admin`, it redirects to `/`.
    *   **UI Level:** Entry points are gated by checking if the user's role is in `['admin', 'owner', 'moderator']` (for general moderation) or specifically `['admin', 'owner']` for the full Admin Panel.
    *   **Role Constants:** Roles are strings: `'owner'`, `'admin'`, `'moderator'`, and `'guest'`. The `src/utils/roleUtils.js` file handles display labels (e.g., `'owner'` -> `Godfather`, `'admin'` -> `High Council`, `'moderator'` -> `Guardian`).

### 2. Entry Points to Admin Panel
*   **RoomListPage:** A floating action button (`.rl-admin-float`) redirects to `/admin-panel`.
*   **SettingsSidebar:** A menu item in the sidebar redirects to `/admin-panel` (line 3231).
*   **Navigation:** Typically accessed via these admin-only UI elements which are conditionally rendered based on the user's role.

### 3. Moderation Actions in AdminPanelPage.jsx
Moderation is primarily handled via the `AdminBanKickModal` component.

| Action | Function Name (in AdminPanelPage) | Firestore Collection / Path |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Ban** | `onConfirm` (passed to modal) | `users/{uid}` (sets `isBanned: true`, `banInfo`) |
| **Kick** | `onConfirm` (passed to modal) | `rooms/{roomId}/kickedUsers/{uid}` |
| **Mute** | `onConfirm` (passed to modal) | `users/{uid}` (sets `mutedInfo.isMuted: true`) |
| **Unban** | `onConfirm` | `users/{uid}` (sets `isBanned: false`) |
| **Unkick** | `handleUnkick` | `rooms/{roomId}/kickedUsers/{uid}` (deletes doc) |
| **Unmute** | `handleUnmute` | `users/{uid}` (sets `mutedInfo.isMuted: false`) |
| **IP Ban** | `IPBanSystem.banUserWithIP` | `bannedIPs/{ip}` |
| **Device Ban**| `confirmDeviceBan` | `bannedDevices/{deviceId}` |

### 4. User List & Online Tracking
*   **User List Loading:** The user list is loaded via an `onSnapshot` listener on the `users` collection in `AdminPanelPage.jsx` (line 225), limited to the first 100 users.
*   **Online Users:** 
    *   The project uses a global `window.onlineUsers` (a `Set` of UIDs) maintained in `HomePage.jsx` via a Realtime Database (RTDB) listener on the `status` node.
    *   `AdminPanelPage.jsx` also listens to `rtdb.ref('status')` to update local `onlineStatuses`.
    *   Presence is tracked at `status/{uid}` in RTDB.

### 5. Icon Library
*   The project **does not use a standard library** like Lucide or React Icons. 
*   Instead, it uses **custom SVG components** and a centralized internal SVG path library found in:
    *   `src/components/AdminBanKickModal.jsx` (the `D` object containing path strings).
    *   `src/utils/toastIcons.jsx` (custom SVG functions).
    *   `src/pages/RoomListPage.jsx` (internal `Ico` component with custom paths).

### 6. Role Hierarchy Summary
*   **Owner/Superowner:** Full access, can change roles and delete users.
*   **Admin:** Full access to Admin Panel, moderation, and room management.
*   **Moderator:** Access to `AdminBanKickModal` for kick/mute/ban but typically restricted from full Admin Panel dashboard settings.
*   **Guest:** Restricted to `'guest'` role, identified by `isGuest: true` in Firestore or local storage. (Display labels: `Purush`, `Stree`, `Navrang`).

arguments.content:
---
name: Emoji-free select pattern
description: How to remove emoji from a native select while keeping SVG icons, for premium/emoji-free UI requirements
---

Native HTML `<option>` elements cannot render `<svg>` or arbitrary markup — only plain text. So when a design requirement says "no emoji, use SVG icons instead" for a dropdown (severity picker, target picker, etc.), you cannot just swap emoji characters for SVG inside `<option>`.

**How to apply:** Replace the `<select>` with a custom button that opens a list/menu of buttons, each rendering an SVG icon + label, wired to the same state setter the `<select>` used. Keep the visual footprint (size, position, click target) consistent with the original design system. This pattern was used for severity and send-to pickers in `WarningAnnouncementModal.jsx`/`WarningAnnouncementManager.jsx`.

arguments.content:
---
name: Owner vs admin panel split
description: How admin console access is split between Owner-only full panel and Owner+Admin moderation-only panel
---

`AdminPanelPage.jsx` (route `/admin-panel`) is restricted to `role === 'owner'` only — admins, moderators, and everyone else are redirected away, both via internal role check and by hiding all navigation entry points (RoomListPage floating button, SettingsSidebar "Admin Controls" section).

Owner + Admin moderation needs (ban/kick/mute users) are served by a separate, lightweight page: `src/pages/BanKickMutePanel.jsx` (route `/mod-panel`). It reuses the *exact same* Firestore/RTDB moderation logic as AdminPanelPage (same `users/{uid}` fields, `rooms/{roomId}/kickedUsers/{uid}`, `IPBanSystem`, and the shared `AdminBanKickModal` component) rather than duplicating collections or adding new listeners — it just runs its own capped `users` query and reuses the RTDB `status` presence tree.

**Why:** the app's moderation escalation ladder distinguishes "full admin console" (owner-only: user role changes, coin/economy tools, feedback replies, device bans, etc.) from "day-to-day moderation" (ban/kick/mute, which admins should also be able to do). Splitting into two pages avoids either over-granting owner-level admin console access to admins, or blocking admins from routine moderation.

**How to apply:** when adding new owner-only tooling to AdminPanelPage, do NOT loosen its role check back to include admin. If admins need a subset of a new capability, extract it into BanKickMutePanel.jsx (or a similar dedicated page) instead.
